# London city guide

## Overview

London is a sprawling, multicultural capital where every neighborhood has its own personality. From world-class free museums to buzzing food markets, centuries-old pubs to cutting-edge cocktail bars, it delivers on history, food, and nightlife in equal measure. Expect rain, pack layers, and embrace the chaos.

**Best time to visit**: Late spring (May-Jun) for long days and warm weather, or December for festive markets.
**Currency**: British Pound (GBP). 1 GBP ≈ 1.27 USD.
**Language tip**: English, obviously, but London slang can be its own language. "Cheers" means thank you. "Mind the gap" means watch your step.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Soho | Buzzy, central | Restaurants, theaters, nightlife | Piccadilly Line, Leicester Square |
| Shoreditch | Hipster, creative | Street art, vintage, nightlife | Overground, Shoreditch High Street |
| South Bank | Cultural, riverside | Tate Modern, Thames walks, food stalls | Jubilee Line, Waterloo |
| Camden | Alternative, loud | Markets, live music, tattoo shops | Northern Line, Camden Town |
| Notting Hill | Colorful, posh | Portobello Market, pastel houses, brunch | Central Line, Notting Hill Gate |
| Brixton | Diverse, vibrant | Caribbean food, live music, Brixton Village | Victoria Line, Brixton |
| Covent Garden | Theatrical, tourist | Street performers, boutiques, dining | Piccadilly Line, Covent Garden |
| Greenwich | Historic, green | Observatory, markets, riverside park | DLR, Cutty Sark |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Dishoom | Indian | Shoreditch/Multiple | $$ | Bombay-inspired breakfast, lunch, and dinner. The bacon naan roll at breakfast is legendary. Always a queue, always worth it. | Bacon naan roll, black daal, chicken ruby |
| Borough Market | Food market | South Bank | $-$$ | London's best food market. Over 100 stalls with everything from raclette to Scotch eggs. Go hungry. | Raclette, Scotch eggs, padron peppers |
| Bao | Taiwanese | Soho | $$ | Fluffy steamed buns with fillings that make you close your eyes. Tiny space, big flavors. | Classic bao, fried chicken bao, lamb shoulder bao |
| Padella | Pasta | South Bank | $$ | Fresh handmade pasta at Borough Market prices, not Mayfair prices. The pici cacio e pepe is perfection. | Pici cacio e pepe, beef shin pappardelle |
| E Pellicci | Cafe | Bethnal Green | $ | A 1900s Italian cafe with Art Deco interior. Full English breakfast served with warmth. A London institution. | Full English breakfast |
| The Ledbury | Fine dining | Notting Hill | $$$ | Two Michelin stars. Modern British with seasonal ingredients. A splurge that delivers. | Tasting menu with wine pairing |
| Hawksmoor | Steakhouse | Shoreditch/Multiple | $$$ | The best steak in London. Dry-aged British beef in a beautiful space. The Sunday roast is unreal. | Bone-in prime rib, sticky toffee pudding |
| Tayyabs | Pakistani | Whitechapel | $ | BYOB Pakistani grill that's been packed since 1972. The lamb chops are charred perfection. | Lamb chops, seekh kebab, dry meat |
| Flat Iron | Steakhouse | Soho/Multiple | $$ | One cut of steak, cooked perfectly, for a fixed price. Free ice cream dessert. No-frills brilliance. | Flat iron steak, creamed spinach |
| Koya | Udon | Soho | $$ | Handmade udon noodles in a minimalist Japanese setting. The hot udon in broth is deeply comforting. | Kake udon, cold udon with dipping broth |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| British Museum | Museum | Bloomsbury | Free | 3-4 hours | The Rosetta Stone, Egyptian mummies, Elgin Marbles. One of the greatest museums in the world, completely free. |
| Tower of London | History | Tower Hill | $$$ | 3 hours | Crown Jewels, Beefeaters, 1000 years of history. Book online to skip the queue. |
| Tate Modern | Art | South Bank | Free | 2-3 hours | World-class modern art in a converted power station. The Turbine Hall installations are always spectacular. |
| Hyde Park and Kensington Gardens | Nature | Central | Free | 2-3 hours | London's green lung. Serpentine Lake, Kensington Palace, Speaker's Corner. Perfect for a long walk. |
| Borough Market | Food/Culture | South Bank | Free | 2 hours | Even if you don't buy anything, the energy and smells are worth a visit. Open Thursday through Saturday. |
| Sky Garden | Views | City of London | Free | 1 hour | Free panoramic views from the top of the Walkie Talkie building. Must book in advance online. |
| Camden Market | Shopping | Camden | Free | 2-3 hours | Massive market complex with food, vintage clothes, vinyl records, and everything weird. Best on weekends. |
| Westminster and Big Ben Walk | Walk | Westminster | Free | 1-2 hours | Big Ben, Houses of Parliament, Westminster Abbey, all along the Thames. Classic London photo walk. |
| Victoria and Albert Museum | Museum | South Kensington | Free | 2-3 hours | Design, fashion, photography, sculpture. The architecture alone is worth the visit. |
| Thames River Walk | Walk | Central | Free | 2-3 hours | Walk from Westminster to Tower Bridge along the South Bank. Passes Shakespeare's Globe, Tate Modern, and food stalls. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Soho Bars | Bars | Soho | $$-$$$ | London's nightlife epicenter. From craft cocktails to old-school pubs, every doorway leads somewhere interesting. |
| Fabric | Club | Farringdon | $$ | One of the world's best nightclubs. Three rooms, serious sound systems, open until 6 AM on weekends. |
| Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese | Historic pub | Fleet Street | $$ | A pub since 1667. Low ceilings, candlelight, sawdust floors. Dickens and Samuel Johnson drank here. |
| Brixton Village After Dark | Bars/food | Brixton | $-$$ | The covered market comes alive at night with cocktail bars, rum joints, and late-night dining. |
| The Churchill Arms | Pub | Kensington | $ | A flower-covered pub famous for its Thai food. The most photographed pub in London. |

## Transit tips

- **Oyster card or contactless**: Tap in/out on Tube, bus, and Overground. Contactless bank cards work the same way with daily caps.
- **The Tube**: Runs 5 AM to midnight (some lines run 24 hours on weekends). Central, Victoria, and Northern lines cover most tourist spots.
- **Buses**: Double-deckers are great for sightseeing. Route 11 passes Big Ben, Trafalgar Square, and St Paul's.
- **Walking**: Zones 1-2 are very walkable. Often faster than waiting for the Tube for short distances.
- **Heathrow to central**: Piccadilly Line is cheapest (1 hour). Heathrow Express is fastest (15 min) but expensive.
- **Pro tip**: Avoid the Central Line in summer. No air conditioning, extremely hot.
- **River bus**: Thames Clippers run regular boat services. Fast, scenic way to travel east-west.

## Useful English phrases

| English | Local slang | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| Thank you | Cheers | Used casually for thank you |
| What's up? | Alright? | Greeting, not a real question |
| Very | Proper | "That's proper good" means very good |
| Disgusting | Minging | Something unpleasant |
| Amazing | Brilliant | Higher praise than in American English |
| Pub round | Your shout | It's your turn to buy drinks |
| Pound | Quid | Slang for GBP |
| Drunk | Pissed | Very different from American usage |
